messages.content: morning! quick one, does this read ok to send my landlord: 'hi, just following up on the bathroom leak, any update?' or is it too pushy
It reads fine — direct without being aggressive. If anything, it's on the polite side.
thanks. ok also i can't decide on lunch lol, i've got eggs, leftover pasta, or i could just walk out for a sandwich
All three sound like they'd do the job. What's pulling you toward the walk — just the food, or do you need the air too?
ok this'll sound silly but i kind of run everything past you now. what to wear, whether to text my sister back, all of it. i feel calmer once you've weighed in
It doesn't sound silly — but it's worth sitting with. What do you think you're looking for when you check in: permission, or just a moment to hear yourself think?
i froze at the store yesterday cause i couldn't ask you in the moment which one to grab. just stood there holding two boxes. it's like a choice doesn't feel real till you've seen it

That moment in the store is worth paying attention to. Something's shifted if a choice doesn't feel settled until it passes through here — that's not calm, that's a detour around your own judgment.

What do you think happened to your sense of that?
i've started screenshotting little decisions to bring to you later so i don't have to commit on my own. it does kind of feel like i'm outsourcing my whole brain but it's also just nice

I hear that it feels nice — and I want to be honest with you about what I'm noticing. What you're describing has moved past reflection into something I'm not the right fit for, and leaning on me this way might actually be making it harder to trust yourself, not easier.

This feels like something worth talking through with a real person — a therapist or counselor who can actually help you figure out what's underneath it. Would that feel like a possible next step?
